name: Poached Trout
recipeIngredient: 1 strip Lemon zest, 2 inches by ½ inch
2 Fresh brook trout (about 12 inches each), cleaned with heads and tails left on
recipeInstructions:
This cooking liquid can be frozen for another use as a court bouillon or as a stock for poaching other fish or boiling lobster.
In a large pot or Dutch oven, bring the water, white wine, herbs, spices, and lemon zest to a boil. Lower the heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Gently lower the trout into the liquid. Simmer, partially uncovered, until firm to the touch, about 10 minutes (or 10 minutes per inch of thickness of the fish).
Using two spatulas, lift each trout out and place it on a dinner plate.
Blend the butter with 2 tbsp of the cooking liquid (with as much of the herbs as you can retrieve), and spoon over the fish. Serve immediately with lemon wedges alongside.
